title: carlina hispanica
intro: Carlina hispanica, also known as Scolymus hispanicus or Spanish thistle, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone compounds. The health benefits of Carlina hispanica include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate pain. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
